title: [apiv2] filter query parameter can't find specified image
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  ./bin/podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080 &

2.  curl -v -X GET 'http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1.40/libpod/images/json?filters%3Dreference%3Ddocker.io%2Flibrary%2Falpine%3Alatest'
    or 
    curl -v -X GET "http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1.40/libpod/images/json?filters=reference=docker.io/library/alpine:latest"|


**Describe the results you received:**
```
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ ./bin/podman images
REPOSITORY                                TAG     IMAGE ID      CREATED       SIZE
docker.io/library/alpine                  latest  f70734b6a266  4 weeks ago   5.88 MB
registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8           latest  8e0b0194b7e1  4 weeks ago   211 MB
registry.access.redhat.com/rhel7/rsyslog  latest  1a140c03afd9  2 months ago  236 MB

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ curl -v -X GET 'http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1.40/libpod/images/json?filters%3Dreference%3Ddocker.io%2Flibrary%2Falpine%3Alatest'|jq|grep ID
Note: Unnecessary use of -X or --request, GET is already inferred.
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8080...

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ curl -v -X GET "http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1.40/libpod/images/json?filters=reference=docker.io/library/alpine:latest"|jq|grep ID
Note: Unnecessary use of -X or --request, GET is already inferred.

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$                                                         
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Can find specified image by filter query parameter

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.0-dev
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.2
Git Commit:   1077d2d0b72d027a01080c0d3ebdb7d99a969661

OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@chuanchang Is this still an issue?

@ParkerVR PTAL @baude PTAL

> 
> 
> @chuanchang Is this still an issue?
> 
> @ParkerVR PTAL @baude PTAL

I still can't find specified images by filter query parameter.

@chuanchang you seem to have a malformed request. Try 'http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1.40/images/json?filter=alpine:latest' instead.

➜  ~ curl -v -X GET 'http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1.40/images/json?filter=alpine:latest' 
Note: Unnecessary use of -X or --request, GET is already inferred.
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8080...
* Connected to 127.0.0.1 (127.0.0.1) port 8080 (#0)
> GET /v1.40/images/json?filter=alpine:latest HTTP/1.1
> Host: 127.0.0.1:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
> 
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Content-Type: application/json
< Date: Thu, 06 Aug 2020 14:41:10 GMT
< Content-Length: 547
< 
[{"Id":"a24bb4013296f61e89ba57005a7b3e52274d8edd3ae2077d04395f806b63d83e","RepoTags":["docker.io/library/alpine:latest"],"Created":1590787186,"Size":5849195,"Names":["docker.io/library/alpine:latest"],"Digest":"sha256:185518070891758909c9f839cf4ca393ee977ac378609f700f60a771a2dfe321","Digests":["sha256:185518070891758909c9f839cf4ca393ee977ac378609f700f60a771a2dfe321","sha256:a15790640a6690aa1730c38cf0a440e2aa44aaca9b0e8931a9f2b0d7cc90fd65"],"History":["a24bb4013296f61e89ba57005a7b3e52274d8edd3ae2077d04395f806b63d83e","\u003cmissing\u003e"]}]
* Connection #0 to host 127.0.0.1 left intact

@ParkerVR thanks! although the above steps works, but I can't find this kind of usage in [http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/libpodListImages](http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/libpodListImages),  and I have a little bit confuse for how to use other filter query parameters like 'id', 'reference' etc.

@zhangguanzhang Interested in opening a PR to fix this?

seem need to url encode,  `filters={"reference":["docker.io/library/alpine:latest"]}`
```
curl -v 'http://localhost:8081/v1.40/libpod/images/json?filters=%7B%22reference%22%3A%5B%22docker.io%2Flibrary%2Falpine%3Alatest%22%5D%7D'
```

@chuanchang must use a full ID
```
    "Id": "c96f8b6bc0d9f0277f118cf485a8c302e32cfe05e7d65f6e9a4c106679439d5e",
    "RepoTags": [
      "docker.io/library/postgres:latest"
    ],

```
encode `{"id":["c96f8b6bc0d9f0277f118cf485a8c302e32cfe05e7d65f6e9a4c106679439d5e"]}` to 
`%7B%22id%22%3A%5B%22c96f8b6bc0d9f0277f118cf485a8c302e32cfe05e7d65f6e9a4c106679439d5e%22%5D%7D` and GET
```
$ curl -v 'http://localhost:8081/v1.40/libpod/images/json?filters=%7B%22id%22%3A%5B%22c96f8b6bc0d9f0277f118cf485a8c302e32cfe05e7d65f6e9a4c106679439d5e%22%5D%7D' | jq .
[
  {
    "Id": "c96f8b6bc0d9f0277f118cf485a8c302e32cfe05e7d65f6e9a4c106679439d5e",
    "RepoTags": [
      "docker.io/library/postgres:latest"
    ],
```
